# Google Just Made Pomelli Global 🌍

This is a significant move. Google expanding Pomelli to 170+ countries signals something important: AI-powered tools are no longer being rolled out regionally — they're launching globally from the start.

For **recruiters and HR leaders**, pay attention. Every time Google broadens access to experimental AI tools, it reshapes how talent discovers, learns, and builds skills worldwide. The playing field shifts fast.

For **engineers and tech professionals**, early adoption of emerging platforms isn't optional anymore — it's a competitive advantage.

The companies and individuals who experiment first will lead first.

Explore it at labs.google/pomelli before your competitors do.

**Follow for more updates on AI, tech, and talent trends.**

tweet.author: Google Labs
tweet.text:
Woah. It’s been a little over 2 months since the start of 2026. Here's what we’ve dropped so far:

🚀 @FlowbyGoogle: Our biggest update yet. We’ve redesigned the interface, bringing image generation to the forefront, making it easier to manage assets, and giving you more precise control over your images and videos. We also made improvements to Veo 3.1 and welcomed Nano Banana 2 to the model lineup for even more expressive and consistent outputs. (Also, come say hi on Instagram: @ flowbygoogle!)

🚀 @JulesAgent: A major speed boost, all Jules free-tier users have been upgraded from Gemini 2.5 Pro to Gemini 3 Flash.

🚀 SynthID: We've added the ability to check whether audio is Google AI-generated (in addition to image and video). Generative media content made with Labs products can be uploaded to the Gemini app to see if they've been generated using Google AI. Simply type @ SynthID and ask "Is this AI?"

🚀 @StitchbyGoogle: We leveled up the MCP ecosystem. Users can now get step-by-step MCP client instructions and grab their API key directly from the Exports panel. We also joined the Antigravity MCP store and added new tools, like a coding agent that can ask to edit screens and generate screen variants.

🚀 Project Genie: Introducing an early research prototype powered by @GoogleDeepMind’s Genie 3 model, Project Genie lets users create and explore infinitely diverse worlds. Available to AI Ultra subscribers in the US.

🚀 @NotebookLM: A massive month for mobile. Users can now add customization prompts to Infographics and Slide Decks directly and generate video overviews on the mobile app. Plus, NotebookLM partnered with @Zillow to launch a featured notebook pre-loaded with their extensive home buying resources. Be on the lookout for other exciting partnerships to come!

🚀 Pomelli: Launched ‘Photoshoot,’ allowing users to take a single image of their product and easily create high quality, customized product shots to elevate their marketing.

🚀 Opal: Introduced a new agent step that analyzes a goal, determines the next best step, and automatically calls models and tools to finish a the task, such as Veo for video or web search for research.

🚀 @Producer_AI: Welcomed ProducerAI to Labs! ProducerAI is a creative collaborator, whether writing lyrics, refining a melody, or inventing entirely new genres.

What a start to the year. 2026 is just getting warmed up.

Explore all of our experiments at: labs.google

drafts.draft:
# Google Labs Is Setting the Pace for 2026 — And It's Only March

Google just dropped a staggering lineup of AI updates in barely two months. From Flow's redesigned creative interface and Veo 3.1 improvements, to SynthID expanding AI-generated content verification across audio, image, and video — the trust and transparency layer is maturing fast.

What stands out most: **the infrastructure play.** Stitch leveling up MCP tooling, Jules upgrading free-tier users to Gemini 3 Flash, and Opal introducing autonomous agent steps that chain models together — this isn't incremental. It's ecosystem building.

For anyone in AI, recruiting tech, or enterprise innovation: the velocity of these releases signals where platform expectations are heading.

**Follow for more insights on where AI is actually moving.**

I appreciate you bringing this to me, but I want to be transparent — the tweet "This is awesome - major congrats 🚀" doesn't contain any substantive content, context, or topic I can meaningfully build upon.

There's no indication of **what** is being celebrated, **who** it's directed at, or **which industry** it relates to.

Rather than fabricate context or produce hollow engagement bait, I'd recommend:

- Sharing the **original source** or context behind the congratulations
- Identifying the specific achievement, company, or milestone referenced
- Providing any additional details about the industry focus

With that context, I can craft a genuinely insightful, audience-relevant LinkedIn post that adds real value rather than empty filler.

tweet.author: Google Labs
tweet.text:
As Google’s home for experimental AI innovation, what excites us most is trying new things, learning, and exploring revolutionary ways to bring the power of our AI models directly to you.

Keeping that Labs energy going, starting today, Whisk and ImageFX will begin to join forces with @FlowbyGoogle, integrating your favorite image and video generation features from Google Labs experiments under one roof. We are excited to keep building alongside you, and we can’t wait to see what you all create!

Learn more here: blog.google/innovation-and-a…

drafts.draft:
## Google Is Consolidating Its Creative AI Tools — And That's a Strategic Move

Google is merging Whisk and ImageFX into Flow, bringing image and video generation under one unified platform.

This isn't just a product update — it's a signal. The era of fragmented AI experiments is giving way to **integrated, production-ready creative ecosystems**.

For those of us watching AI's impact on talent acquisition, employer branding, and content at scale, this matters. Unified generative tools lower the barrier for teams to produce compelling visual content — fast.

The companies that learn to leverage these platforms early will have a significant edge in how they attract, engage, and communicate.

The consolidation phase of AI has begun. Pay attention.

**Follow for more updates on AI trends shaping the future of work.** 🚀

tweet.author: Google Labs
tweet.text:
Opal, our no-code visual builder for AI workflows, just got a major upgrade. 🧠💎

We’ve added a new agent step that analyzes your goal, determines the best approach, and automatically calls the right tools — such as Veo for video or web search for research — to complete the task.

We’re also adding new tools to make the agent even more capable:

💾 Memory – Remember info, like a user’s name or your style preferences across sessions.
🚀 Dynamic Routing – Let the agent choose the next best step using the “@ Go to” tool.
💬 Interactive Chat – Initiate user interactions to gather missing information or present options before moving on.

Try it now → opal.google

drafts.draft:
## Google's Opal Update Signals Where No-Code AI is Heading

This is worth paying attention to.

Google just upgraded Opal — their no-code visual builder for AI workflows — with an intelligent agent step that autonomously selects the right tools to complete a task. Think: automatic routing to video generation, web research, or chat interactions based on the goal you define.

The additions that matter most:
🧠 **Persistent memory** across sessions
🔀 **Dynamic routing** — the agent decides its own next step
💬 **Interactive chat** for real-time human-in-the-loop input

For anyone in **recruiting, HR tech, or talent operations**, this is the trajectory. Autonomous agents that remember candidate context, route workflows intelligently, and ask clarifying questions *before* acting — not after.

No-code AI orchestration is no longer experimental. It's infrastructure.

tweet.author: Google Labs
tweet.text:
🚨 Big news: @producer_ai is officially joining Google Labs! 🎶 

ProducerAI is a creative collaborator, whether you’re writing lyrics, developing a melody or experimenting with genres. With ProducerAI, you can turn your imagination into dynamic tracks.

We share ProducerAI’s mission of helping artists of every level make the music they imagine with Lyria 3, and are excited to welcome them to the Labs family. 

Learn more: blog.google/innovation-and-a…

Tools like Pomelli are making it possible to create professional product images from a simple text description or reference upload — no studio, no photographer, no lengthy turnaround.

This matters beyond marketing. Think about how quickly recruiting teams can generate employer branding assets, or how hardware companies can visualize prototype concepts before physical production.

The real shift? **Creative execution is no longer the bottleneck — creative thinking is.**

As generative AI tools mature, the competitive advantage moves from production capability to strategic vision. The teams that win will be the ones who know *what* to create, not just *how*.

With Photoshoot, you can start from a single image of your product and easily create high quality, customized product shots to elevate your marketing. 

Available free of charge in the US, Canada, Australia & New Zealand! Get started with Pomelli today at labs.google/pomelli
